title: Add `--sync` option (TODO 18.1)
body:
This adds a new `--sync` option that makes curl download a resource only if the
remote file is newer than the local copy, based on the `Last-Modified` header.

- Download if no local file exists.
- Download if the server does not send `Last-Modified`.
- Skip transfer (exit code 0) if the local file is newer or equal.
- Applies to HTTP/HTTPS; other protocols fall back to normal behavior.

Tests added: test1586, test1587, test1588, test1589.
Documentation updated in curl.1 and help output.

This feature is fully opt-in and does not affect existing behavior.
Implements TODO item 18.1.
author: peripherium

issue_comments.body:
Analysis of PR #19871 at [8b934ba6](https://github.com/curl/curl/commit/8b934ba61ee7b1570e16dbe2756e228d3f84a0f1):

[Test 1589 failed](https://github.com/curl/curl/actions/runs/20024681271/job/57419240940), which has NOT been flaky recently, so **there could be a real issue in this PR**.  Note that this test has failed in 20 different CI jobs (the link just goes to one of them).

###### Generated by [Testclutch](https://testclutch.curl.se/)

My editor made a few formatting errors. Please excuse the inconvenience. In my opinion, everything is fine now.

Now I'd like to fix the errors that the tests flagged. However, I don't understand what this "complexity test" means.

EDIT: Okay, I understand now. I included too many if statements in the code. However, one question remains: why is this link check failing?

I'm generally skeptical of this command line option.

- this operation can mostly be done already by just crafting the right set of options
- it introduces a state for the command line that makes it hard to add additional URLs/options
- it's not really a feature we see users ask for

See also #19700 which was a recent separate PR for the exact same feature.

I probably would have moved towards removing this item from the TODO if this new PR hadn't appeared...

The last change was intended to fix the deficiencies identified by the code complexity test.

The behavior of these tests is incomprehensible to me. They stop with an output like this:

Error: fatal: No url found for submodule path 'curl_step/curl' in .gitmodules

Error: The process '/usr/bin/git' failed with exit code 128

I'm not experiencing any errors on my machine, and I can't figure out what's causing these tests to fail.

> The last change was intended to fix the deficiencies identified by the code complexity test.
> 
> The behavior of these tests is incomprehensible to me. They stop with an output like this:
> 
> Error: fatal: No url found for submodule path 'curl_step/curl' in .gitmodules
> 
> Error: The process '/usr/bin/git' failed with exit code 128
> 
> I'm not experiencing any errors on my machine, and I can't figure out what's causing these tests to fail.

It's caused by a submodule or something alike declared in a new subdirectory `curl_step`.
It's not part of curl, and deleting it should fix it.

MSVC is complaining about unused/uninit `p` variable and CI is expected to fail
after a rebase on master for missing XML prolog lines in the new tests added.
Copying them from any other test should fix it.

With this and a few other minor changes, the tests no longer produce any errors (except for one). Thanks for the help.

"Windows / dl-mingw, CM 15.1.0-x86_64 schannel (pull_request)" cancelled, and I don't know what the cause is.

> With this and a few other minor changes, the tests no longer produce any errors (except for one). Thanks for the help.
> 
> "Windows / dl-mingw, CM 15.1.0-x86_64 schannel (pull_request)" cancelled, and I don't know what the cause is.

It's a flaky hung job we can ignore.
TIL xmllint is happy with raw `>` (aka `%GT`) in test data.
